summary: Matched the Dolphin DSP task module by deleting asm/dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.s and replacing it with src/dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c, wiring the new C object into obj_files.mk, and adding a header prototype for __DSP_exec_task. The PR body says it was based on SMB decomp. The new C covers DSP task interrupt handling, mailbox handshakes, task execution/booting, priority insertion, and task removal while preserving matching-sensitive details such as explicit polling loops, global DSP task pointers, callback dispatch, magic mailbox constants, and a MUST_MATCH force_active unused string.
what_changed: Removed the 676-line assembly implementation at asm/dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.s.
Added a 313-line C implementation at src/dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c.
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.s.o.
Defined DSP task globals in C: __DSP_curr_task, __DSP_first_task, __DSP_last_task, __DSP_tmp_task, __DSP_rude_task, and __DSP_rude_task_pending.

Implemented __DSPHandler with interrupt context save/restore, DSP mailbox reads, message dispatch on 0xDCD10000 through 0xDCD10004, task state transitions, rude-task handling, and callback invocation.
Implemented __DSP_exec_task to send previous-task DRAM fields and next-task IRAM/DRAM/vector fields to the DSP through DSPSendMailToDSP, with DSPCheckMailToDSP polling after each send.
Implemented __DSP_boot_task with the boot mailbox command sequence and debug prints for task boot parameters.
Implemented __DSP_insert_task as priority-ordered linked-list insertion over DSPTaskInfo prev/next links.
Implemented __DSP_remove_task to clear flags, mark state 3, unlink first/last/middle tasks, and update current/first/last task globals.
smart_moves: Used an existing SMB decomp as a reference, according to the PR body, which is appropriate for matching shared Dolphin SDK code.
Kept mailbox handshakes structurally explicit: every DSPSendMailToDSP call is followed by a while (DSPCheckMailToDSP() != 0) polling loop, mirroring the assembly control flow.

Preserved interrupt-handler context behavior by allocating a local OSContext, calling OSClearContext/OSSetCurrentContext before handling the DSP message, then restoring the incoming context at the end.
Modeled the assembly's large compare/branch tree as a switch over DSP mailbox message constants, making the behavior searchable without losing the message cases.

Used direct DSPTaskInfo field names such as state, flags, init_cb, res_cb, done_cb, req_cb, next, prev, priority, iram_mmem_addr, iram_length, iram_addr, dram_mmem_addr, dram_length, dram_addr, dsp_init_vector, and dsp_resume_vector rather than leaving offset-based logic in assembly.
Preserved a matching-sensitive unused string with #ifdef MUST_MATCH, #pragma force_active, and a static char unused[] for "__DSP_add_task() : Added task    : 0x%08X\n".
decomp_lessons: For Dolphin SDK code, comparing against another decompilation project can provide a strong semantic skeleton, but matching still depends on local details such as build object order, prototypes, globals, and exact polling structure.
When replacing an asm file with C, update obj_files.mk in the same PR so the matched source object replaces the asm object cleanly.

Mailbox-driven hardware code often matches best when written with simple explicit loops and repeated calls rather than abstract helpers, because the original assembly usually has one call/poll sequence per mailbox word.
Interrupt handlers may require local OSContext stack layout and explicit OSClearContext/OSSetCurrentContext calls to match generated code and preserve semantics.
Magic DSP mailbox constants should remain visible in the matched C when no named constants are present in the evidence; inventing enum names would reduce evidence fidelity.
Unused SDK strings may need force_active guards under MUST_MATCH to preserve rodata without affecting non-matching builds.
If a formerly local asm symbol is referenced across C files, expose its prototype in the relevant Dolphin header rather than relying on implicit declarations.
naming_conventions: Internal Dolphin DSP symbols use a double-underscore prefix, e.g. __DSPHandler, __DSP_exec_task, __DSP_boot_task, __DSP_insert_task, __DSP_remove_task.
Global DSP task scheduler state follows __DSP_<role>_task naming, e.g. __DSP_curr_task, __DSP_first_task, __DSP_last_task, __DSP_tmp_task, __DSP_rude_task.
The pending boolean uses the same internal prefix and descriptive suffix: __DSP_rude_task_pending.
Callback fields in DSPTaskInfo use short role names with _cb suffix: init_cb, res_cb, done_cb, req_cb.
Task-link fields use conventional linked-list names prev and next.
The implementation uses concise parameter names a and b in __DSP_exec_task, matching-oriented rather than self-documenting; future cleanup should avoid renaming unless proven safe for matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the deleted assembly labels for __DSPHandler, __DSP_exec_task, __DSP_boot_task, __DSP_insert_task, and __DSP_remove_task into C functions.
Matched hardware register manipulation in __DSPHandler as temp = __DSPRegs[5]; __DSPRegs[5] = (temp & -41) | 0x80.
Used an explicit local pad array and OSContext sp10 in __DSPHandler, likely to influence stack layout; evidence shows u8 pad[8] and OSContext sp10.
Kept DSPCheckMailFromDSP and DSPCheckMailToDSP wait loops as empty continue loops to preserve branch shape.
Represented 0xDCD10002 as 0xDCD10003 when the current task has flag bit 1 set, matching the assembly's pre-switch message rewrite.

Preserved mailbox constants in-place: 0xDCD10000, 0xDCD10001, 0xDCD10002, 0xDCD10003, 0xDCD10004, 0xCDD10001, 0xCDD10002, 0xCDD10003, and boot constants such as 0x80F3A001, 0x80F3C002, 0x80F3A002, 0x80F3B002, 0x80F3D001.
Used casts to u32 for pointer-valued mailbox sends, e.g. dram_mmem_addr and iram_mmem_addr.
Used #ifdef MUST_MATCH with #pragma push, #pragma force_active on, and #pragma pop to keep an otherwise unused static string in the object.
review_feedback: One collaborator review by ribbanya approved the PR.
No review comments or issue comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename DSP mailbox magic constants or introduce enums/macros for 0xDCD100xx and 0xCDD100xx.
Check whether later DSP matching work reuses the SMB decomp reference strategy.
Search for subsequent edits to DSPTaskInfo field names or layout after dsp_task.c landed.
Look for later PRs touching __DSP_boot_task debug prints or the MUST_MATCH unused string.
Compare other Dolphin SDK asm-to-C conversions that update obj_files.mk and expose new internal prototypes in headers.
